user.title: Connect to an Arista switch
user.description: Enter the management address and eAPI credentials for the switch. eAPI must be enabled (`management api http-commands`).

data_description.host: Hostname or management IP address of the switch.
data_description.verify_ssl: Enable only if the switch presents a certificate trusted by Home Assistant. Off by default because switches usually use self-signed certificates.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate with the switch
reauth_confirm.description: The credentials for {host} are no longer valid. Enter new eAPI credentials.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure the Arista switch
reconfigure.description: Update the connection details. The switch serial number must match the existing device.

data_description.host: Hostname or management IP address of the switch.
data_description.verify_ssl: Enable only if the switch presents a trusted certificate.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect. Check the host, port, and that eAPI is enabled.

abort.wrong_device: The credentials are for a different switch than the one configured.

data_description.scan_interval: How often to poll the switch, in seconds.
data_description.monitor_interfaces: Create per-interface link and throughput entities (high entity count).

invalid_auth.message: Authentication with the switch failed; the credentials may have changed.

confirm.title: Environment monitoring unavailable
confirm.description: {name} did not respond to environment commands (`show environment ...`). This is expected on virtual platforms (vEOS/cEOS), which have no physical sensors. Temperature, power, and fan entities will remain unavailable. Confirm to dismiss this notice.
